[STATUS] Accepted
[DATE] 2026-05-02

[DECISION] Ent tier adds raw TCP as a third NCP transport for intra-cluster Anchor→Memory Node traffic.
[DECISION] TCP NCP runs on a separate port (default 17436, configurable).
[DECISION] TCP NCP is not exposed externally; it is a cluster-internal transport only.
[DECISION] External agents always speak HTTP or WebSocket NCP (ADR-001); TCP NCP is an implementation detail of Ent topology.

[RATIONALE] Anchor Node fans out QueryFrames to many Memory Nodes in the hot path — HTTP handshake and WS upgrade latency are measurable at high QPS.
[RATIONALE] Within a container cluster (K8s pod mesh or docker-compose network) raw TCP is safe to use: firewall is at the cluster ingress, not between pods.
[RATIONALE] Persistent TCP connections eliminate per-request TLS handshake overhead (even with HTTP/2 multiplexing, TCP gives lower framing overhead).
[RATIONALE] Tier2 MsgPack encoding (available from Pro WebSocket path) carries over to TCP NCP with no codec changes.

[IMPACT] New project: Banyan.Transport (or Banyan.Anchor includes it) — System.Net.Sockets or Kestrel raw-TCP IConnectionListenerFactory.
[IMPACT] Separate IHostedService: TcpNcpListener starts on BANYAN_NCP_TCP_PORT (default 17436).
[IMPACT] NID auth: first frame on each new TCP connection is an IdentFrame (same as WS path); connection is rejected if verification fails.
[IMPACT] Separate prometheus metrics: ncp_tcp_connections_active, ncp_tcp_frames_rx/tx — distinct from HTTP and WS counters.
[IMPACT] Deployment: new port 17436 must be opened between Anchor and Memory Node pods; must NOT be exposed at cluster ingress.
[IMPACT] Ops runbook must document: connection draining on graceful shutdown, reconnect backoff for Anchor Node clients.

[CONTEXT] Raw TCP NCP is only justified at Ent scale where Anchor Node→Memory Node traffic volume is high.
[CONTEXT] Pro tier uses WebSocket NCP (ADR-001) which is sufficient for Pro's N Memory Node fan-out.
[CONTEXT] If NPS.NWP eventually ships a native TCP transport, this implementation becomes replaceable with upstream — the BanyanMemoryProvider interface is unchanged.
[CONTEXT] K-of-N CA quorum messages (also Ent) should use the same TCP NCP path to avoid a separate channel.
